title: American apple pie
subtitle: Double-crust pie with spiced apple filling
excerpt: A deep-dish apple pie with a flaky double shortcrust, a thick spiced apple filling, and a crimped top crust. All-butter pastry, Bramley apples, cinnamon. The filling is thickened with flour to set when sliced.
sourceNotes: American apple pie is documented in domestic cookery from the colonial period, brought by English settlers and adapted through the nineteenth century into the double-crust form. The all-butter, flaky pastry and cinnamon-spiced filling is the standard formula. Bramley apples replace American pie varieties in this UK version.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze unbaked. Bake from frozen at 200°C fan for 1 hour 15 minutes.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: The pastry dough keeps refrigerated for 2 days. The baked pie keeps for 3 days.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven, lower-middle shelf — the base needs heat from below to cook through

glossaryTerms.definition: Slits cut through the top crust of a double-crust pie before baking. Steam from the cooking filling must escape; without vents it builds pressure under the pastry, making it soggy or tearing through unpredictably. Four or five 3 cm cuts arranged around the centre are sufficient.

content.text: A double-crust pie has two challenges: keeping the pastry flaky and making sure the base bakes through. Both are solved by the same technique — cold butter in the pastry and a hot lower oven shelf. The
in the top crust are functional: the apple filling produces a lot of steam and needs somewhere to go.

Mix the {{plain-flour}} (for the pastry), {{fine-sea-salt}}, and {{caster-sugar}} (for the pastry) in a large bowl. Add the cold {{unsalted-butter}} and rub in quickly until the mixture resembles rough crumbs with some pea-sized pieces of butter visible. Add 5–7 tablespoons of ice-cold water, one at a time, mixing with a fork until the dough just comes together. Divide into two equal pieces, flatten into discs, and refrigerate for 30 minutes.
Toss the {{bramley-apples}} with the {{caster-sugar}} (for the filling), {{plain-flour}} (for thickening), {{ground-cinnamon}}, {{ground-nutmeg}}, and {{lemon-juice}}.

Heat the oven to 200°C fan. Roll one pastry disc on a floured surface to 3 mm thick. Line the pie dish, leaving 2 cm overhang. Fill with the apple mixture — pile it high, it will cook down. Roll the second disc and lay over the top. Press the edges together, trim, and crimp. Cut four vents in the top.

Brush the top with the beaten {{free-range-eggs}} and sprinkle with {{demerara-sugar}}. Bake on the lower-middle shelf at 200°C fan for 20 minutes, then reduce to 180°C fan and bake for a further 30–35 minutes until the pastry is deep golden and the filling is bubbling through the vents. Rest for at least 1 hour before slicing.
